question: A company tracks annual copies of a board game sold. According to the chart, which statement about the most recent year is appropriate?

options.A: The most recent year has the lowest annual copies sold in the entire period.
options.B: The most recent year shows a sharp drop in performance compared with earlier years.
options.C: Because the most recent year only includes sales up to June 1, we cannot draw conclusions about its full-year trend.
options.D: The most recent year proves that copies sold will continue to decline in the future.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (451 copies) directly to full-year values like 2,544 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
